name: Chicago'S Bravest
description_short: State-chartered credit union serving Chicago firefighters and residents since 1957, offering savings, auto loans, personal loans, and mortgages with $10.2M in assets.
description_long:
Chicago's Bravest Credit Union was established in 1957 and is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois. The institution operates as a state-chartered, not-for-profit credit union with a primary mission to serve the Chicago community, particularly those with ties to the fire department. With over 60 years of operational history, the credit union has built a stable membership base in one of the nation's largest cities.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including regular savings accounts, share draft accounts, money market accounts, share certificates (CDs), auto loans (new and used vehicles), personal loans, mortgages, and online bill pay. Members have access to online banking and mobile apps for both iOS and Android devices. The institution provides financial calculators for mortgages and auto loans to help members estimate payments. Customer service is available by phone at (773) 445-1700, and the main office is located at 10231 S Western Avenue in Chicago.

Chicago's Bravest distinguishes itself as a not-for-profit institution, which typically allows it to offer competitive rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks. The credit union maintains a dedicated staff of over 5 employees and serves more than 1,100 loyal members. Their routing number is 271078007. The institution monitors service status for online banking, mobile app, and traditional services, with members able to report outages or service issues.

However, prospective members should note that this is a very small credit union with only one branch and zero ATMs, which significantly limits accessibility and convenience. The lobby is currently closed according to the website. With only $10.2M in assets and 1,100 members, this institution offers limited scale compared to larger financial institutions, and membership eligibility appears restricted to Illinois residents under their state charter. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Not-for-profit structure typically offers better rates and lower fees than for-profit banks
Competitive auto loan rates (4.71% for new vehicles according to listed rates)
Offers full suite of loan products including mortgages, auto loans, and personal loans
Online banking and mobile app access for both iOS and Android platforms
Online bill pay capability for members
Over 60 years of operational history since 1957 demonstrates stability
Provides financial calculators (mortgage and auto loan) to help members estimate costs
cons: Only 1 branch location with 0 ATMs severely limits accessibility and convenience
Very small institution ($10.2M assets, 1,100 members) means limited resources and service capacity
Lobby currently closed according to website, limiting in-person service options
No reviews currently available on the platform, making it difficult to assess member satisfaction
Membership appears restricted to Illinois residents; unclear if broader membership eligibility exists
best_for: Chicago-area residents with easy access to the single Western Avenue branch location
Individuals seeking a not-for-profit alternative to traditional banks with better rates
Members of the Chicago fire department or those with community ties to the institution
diagnosis: Chicago's Bravest Credit Union is best for Chicago-area residents seeking not-for-profit banking with competitive rates on loans, particularly those with convenient access to the single Western Avenue location. The main caveat is severe operational limitations: with only one branch, zero ATMs, a currently-closed lobby, and just $10.2M in assets, this institution offers minimal accessibility and service capacity compared to mainstream financial institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
